org.key_project.util.collection

Interface ImmutableSet<T>

  • All Superinterfaces:
    Iterable<T>, Serializable
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    DefaultImmutableSet, DefaultImmutableSet.NILSet

    public interface ImmutableSet<T>
extends Iterable<T>, Serializable
    interface implemented by non-destructive Sets. CONVENTION: Each SetOf implementation has to offer a public static final variable .nil()

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      ImmutableSet<T> add(T element)
      adds an element
      ImmutableSet<T> addUnique(T element)
      adds an element, barfs if the element is already present
      boolean contains(T obj) 
      boolean equals(Object o) 
      int hashCode() 
      ImmutableSet<T> intersect(ImmutableSet<T> set) 
      boolean isEmpty() 
      Iterator<T> iterator() 
      ImmutableSet<T> remove(T element) 
      int size() 
      boolean subset(ImmutableSet<T> s) 
      <S> S[] toArray(S[] array)
      Convert the set to a Java array
      ImmutableSet<T> union(ImmutableSet<T> set) 

      • Methods inherited from interface java.lang.Iterable

        forEach, spliterator

    • Method Detail

      • add

        ImmutableSet<T> add(T element)
        adds an element

      • union

        ImmutableSet<T> union(ImmutableSet<T> set)
        Returns:
        union of this set with set

        ImmutableSet<T> intersect(ImmutableSet<T> set)
        Returns:
        intersection of this set with set

        Iterator<T> iterator()
        Specified by:
        iterator in interface Iterable<T>
        Returns:
        Iterator of the set

      • contains

        boolean contains(T obj)
        Returns:
        true iff obj in set

      • subset

        boolean subset(ImmutableSet<T> s)
        Returns:
        true iff this set is subset of set s

      • size

        int size()
        Returns:
        int the cardinality of the set

      • isEmpty

        boolean isEmpty()
        Returns:
        true iff the set is empty

      • remove

        ImmutableSet<T> remove(T element)
        Returns:
        set without element

      • equals

        boolean equals(Object o)
        Overrides:
        equals in class Object
        Returns:
        true iff the this set is subset of o and vice versa.

      • hashCode

        int hashCode()
        Overrides:
        hashCode in class Object

      • addUnique

        ImmutableSet<T> addUnique(T element)
                   throws NotUniqueException
        adds an element, barfs if the element is already present
        Parameters:
        element - of type that has to be added to this set
        Throws:
        org.key_project.utils.collection.NotUniqueException - if the element is already present
        NotUniqueException

      • toArray

        <S> S[] toArray(S[] array)
        Convert the set to a Java array
